mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration and weak consumer spending, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real stock of assets indicates shifting wealth effects; when GDPC1 growth outpaces RSAFS growth, corporate earnings expectations rise, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a quarterly return regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines during monetary tightening cycles reflect supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, which compresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) outside tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3% annually, elevated federal funds rates fail to anchor expectations, causing real rates to turn negative and triggering flight-to-quality flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows no predictive power for 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL > 3.0%, or returns are positive in > 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor demand and stronger consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day SPX returns over a 4-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.4 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential construction demand and related supply chains, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of months where HOUST falls below 1400.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and rising unemployment confirm a broad-based economic slowdown, prompting equity risk premium repricing that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and UNRATE fails to predict 5-day returns (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression, or positive returns occur in > 58% of co-declining observations.
